title: Add(): learn to record hashes of what we add
body:
Add a field to `AddOrCopyOptions` that can take a 1hash.Hash1, or more often a `Hash` returned by `digest.Digester`'s `Hash()` method, to calculate a sum over what we add or copy.

Make the help output summarizing the arguments that `buildah add` and `buildah copy` accept more closely match their man pages.

comments.body:
LGTM
but failing tests.
Is this for the --layers work?

Changes LGTM, seeing new related warnings in the test output:

```
add.go:39:63:warning: hasher can be io.Writer (interfacer)
util.go:95:62:warning: hasher can be io.Writer (interfacer)
util.go:111:58:warning: hasher can be io.Writer (interfacer)
util.go:127:56:warning: hasher can be io.Writer (interfacer)
```

@TomSweeneyRedHat I tried to lower buildah default logging to Warning, but it looks like that was a mistake, since we have stuff like this showing up to the user. I think we need to move it back to Errors before we release.
@rhatdan, ugh hadn't thought of that.  If we bump back up to error, will that mess up Podman?
No.  But there was some Warnings I wanted to get out to buildah.
Replaced `hash.Hash` objects with `io.Writer` in the new additions, and tweaked things so that `copyFileWithTar` produces a digest of just the file contents based on feedback from @umohnani8.
